story: AI vs salmon bycatch: the nets have eyes now (Sullivan bill July 17 hook + NOAA YOLOv11 + SalmonVision tribal weirs)
in_window_peg: Sullivan 'revamped' Bycatch Reduction and Research Act, KUCB 2026-07-17 (Maggie Nelson); original bill Jan 7, 2026 (Sullivan, Murkowski, Begich)

numbers_allowed_on_screen.value: 99.3% accuracy predicting salmon presence on full tows
numbers_allowed_on_screen.label: per National Fisherman

numbers_allowed_on_screen.value: $200,000 USFWS Tribal Wildlife Grant, Sitka Tribe of Alaska, Redoubt Lake
numbers_allowed_on_screen.label: USFWS, announced Oct 16, 2024

numbers_allowed_on_screen.value: SalmonVision: initial mAP 67.6%; coho >90%; sockeye >80%; 2.5M annotated frames; 16 sites, goal 100 watersheds by 2028
numbers_allowed_on_screen.label: per SalmonVision project (reported, blog source) - MEDIUM confidence

numbers_allowed_on_screen.value: Bill introduced Jan 7, 2026; revamped version reported July 17, 2026
numbers_allowed_on_screen.label: KUCB / Sullivan Senate office

quotes_allowed.text: There is a real and urgent need to empower local and indigenous communities with in season information on the status of salmon populations returning to their watersheds.
quotes_allowed.speaker: Dr. William Atlas, Wild Salmon Center
quotes_allowed.label: reported (single source) - MEDIUM confidence

quotes_allowed.text: What takes a trained observer days or weeks can be run by the model in a matter of hours.
quotes_allowed.speaker: National Fisherman (Carli Clancy), describing NOAA's model
quotes_allowed.label: per National Fisherman

quotes_banned_as_written: Sullivan KUCB quote (contains two em dashes) - paraphrase only, no direct on-screen use unless re-punctuated
'Models can process fishing tows in hours...' - NOT verbatim, do not use
cut_unverifiable: EFP #2025-01 renewal / Sept 30 2026 authorization / permit-holder names - NOAA page 404. NO on-screen use.
Pilot Station sonar 102,756 Yukon Chinook count - unverifiable, CUT
B-season June 10 open date - soften to 'the summer season is underway'
'1 hour of footage per ~1 minute' SalmonVision throughput - CUT
'electronic monitoring/reporting' as a bill provision - appears in neither source, CUT
'real-time' on genetic stock ID - DROP the 'real-time'
'tribe stepped up after federal cuts' causal framing - blog-only, DO NOT assert

context_verified.sentence: Chinook salmon on the Yukon River have been in a prolonged period of low productivity, which has caused significant hardship for residents of the Yukon River drainage.
context_verified.label: ADFG (primary, standing management page)

context_verified.sentence: In April 2024, the Alaska Department of Fish and Game and Fisheries and Oceans Canada signed an agreement aimed at the recovery of Yukon River Chinook salmon.
context_verified.label: ADFG press release Apr 1, 2024

context_verified.sentence: Dutch Harbor, Alaska has been the leading US port for seafood volume for more than 25 consecutive years.
context_verified.label: NOAA Fisheries of the United States

context_verified.sentence: 'Bering Sea pollock = largest US fishery by volume'
context_verified.status: LABELED ONLY - say 'one of the largest fisheries in the world, per NOAA' instead

context_verified.sentence: Any specific 2026 in-season Yukon Chinook figure

fairness_must_include: NOAA's YOLOv11 is RESEARCH showing promise, NOT deployed real-time on deck; needs more Chinook-heavy testing before operational use (NOAA's own framing).
Trawl-sector fair point: salmon bycatch is incidental and capped; Western Alaska salmon declines are driven substantially by ocean/climate conditions, not bycatch alone.

Sitka Tribe of Alaska = federally recognized tribal government; frame Redoubt Lake work as tribal-led self-determination (grant title: 'Artificial Intelligence for Subsistence Salmon Monitoring and Management').
